entries.definition: Young Stark daughter, now disguised as an orphan boy 'Arry' while traveling with Yoren's group to the Wall. Formerly called 'Arya Horseface' and 'Lumpyhead' by other children.
Black brother of the Night's Watch who recruits from the dungeons. Currently transporting men and boys to the Wall, including Arya in disguise.
Arya's castle-forged steel sword, given to her by Jon Snow. A slender blade suitable for her water dancer training.
Orphan boy, former baker's apprentice who lost his mother. Fat, sunburned, with straw-colored hair. Killed a boy by kicking him and now travels with Yoren's group.
Orphan boy who stole before being caught. His arms are mottled green to the elbow from his work as a dyer's apprentice. Named Arya 'Lumpyhead'.
Older orphan boy with shaggy black hair, big for his age with a broad chest. Wears a horned helm he polishes constantly but never wears. Protective of Arya.
Arya's ancestral home, the Stark seat in the North. She wishes to return there instead of continuing to the Wall.
The capital city where Arya's father was executed. She left disguised as a boy, not looking back except wishing it would wash away.
Ancient ice fortress where the Night's Watch serves. Yoren is transporting recruits there.
Order of black-cloaked guardians holding the Wall. Yoren recruits men from the dungeons to join them.
Arya's father, Lord of Winterfell, who was executed in King's Landing. Yoren was supposed to take him to the Wall before something 'went queer'.
Arya's half-brother (bastard son of Lord Eddard). He gave Arya her sword Needle. Arya wishes to meet him at the Wall.
The queen of King's Landing, who was looking for Arya after her father's death. Lannister guardsmen serve her.
The prince whom Arya blames for her father's death. She wishes to see him punished.
Arya's elder sister, still trapped in King's Landing. Arya worries about her fate.
Arya's elder brother, whom she hopes to see again if she returns to Winterfell.
Arya's younger brother at Winterfell, whom she hopes to see again.
Arya's youngest brother at Winterfell.
A blood-red comet crossing the sky, which Arya sees as resembling her father's greatsword Ice after execution. Called 'The Red Sword' by the Bull.
Legendary ripply metal of ancient Valyria. Arya's father's greatsword Ice was made of this rare material.
Lord Eddard's ancient Stark greatsword made of Valyrian steel. Executed with it after his capture. Arya sees the red comet as resembling it.
The King's Justice who executed Lord Eddard by cutting off his head in King's Landing.
Royal executioner position. Ser Ilyn holds this title.
Combat posture taught by Syrio Forel, Arya's dancing master in King's Landing. Used for agile sword fighting.
Arya's water dancing sword instructor in King's Landing, who taught her the Braavosi fighting style.
Noble House of Winterfell, whose sigil is the direwolf. Arya identifies as 'a Stark of Winterfell'.
Ancient wolf symbol of House Stark. Arya reminds herself that 'direwolves don't cry' to endure punishment.
Tobacco-like leaf chewed by Northmen. Yoren gave Arya some to help with the sting of his whipping; it tastes foul.
Term for members of the Night's Watch, who wear black cloaks. Yoren is referred to as one.
poorest district of King's Landing, mentioned in Arya's thoughts about the city.
Royal castle in King's Landing where the Iron Throne is located.
Major sept (temple) in King's Landing where Arya's father was executed.
Temple of the Seven Gods. Arya remembers the paving stones near the sept where her father died.
Ancient road connecting King's Landing to Winterfell and continuing to the Wall.
Mountain range in the Vale, home to wild men who raid the countryside.
Central realm of the Seven Kingdoms, through which the kingsroad passes.
Mountainous realm ruled by House Arryn, protected by the Mountains of the Moon.
Northern realm of the Seven Kingdoms, home to Winterfell and House Stark.
The unified realm of Westeros, comprising multiple regions and houses.
High-quality steel forged in a castle smithy, superior to common iron. Needle is made of this material.
Warhorse of medium size, finer than a plow horse. Yoren bought coursers for the boys.
Bound with chains or shackles, typically on hands and feet. The worst prisoners were kept fettered in Yoren's wagon.
Laughed or spoke loudly and harshly, like a donkey's cry. Lommy brayed when he laughed.
In a gloomy, resentful, and silent manner. Arya chewed her lip sullenly when mocked.
Moved sideways or shied, as a horse does when startled. Hot Pie's donkey hawed and bucked when struck.
Jumped or leaped with agility. Arya vaulted off her donkey to defend herself.
Flowing or falling in drops. Blood dribbled from Hot Pie's broken nose.
Dirty and sordid; bleak. (Note: This word appears in narration describing conditions.)
